evals.name: b2b-saas-email-bison-with-strategy
evals.prompt:
Write a cold email sequence for PostPilot, a B2B SaaS tool that automates social media scheduling and analytics for marketing agencies.

Strategy context (from strategy skill output):
- ICP: Marketing agency owners/founders, 5-50 employees, US
- Pain points: Spending 6+ hours/week manually scheduling posts across client accounts, inconsistent posting killing engagement metrics, no unified analytics dashboard
- Offer: $149/mo starter plan, automates scheduling across 8+ client accounts
- Value lever: Save time
- Case study: 12-person agency went from posting 3x/week to daily across 8 client accounts, saving 15 hours/week
- Messaging angles: Pain Dagger (time spent scheduling), Proof Machine (15hr/week savings), Value Gift (free workflow audit)
- Tone: 2/5 formality, casual, direct, founder-to-founder
- CTA strategy: Book a call
- Front-end offer: Free workflow audit of their current scheduling setup
- Sequencer: Email Bison
- Sender: Sarah Chen, Founder
- Campaign name: postpilot-agencies
evals.expected_output: 4-email sequence with Email Bison spintax, subject lines, bodies under 100 words, proper signature token, CTA progression from permission-based to breakup
expectations: Exactly 4 emails produced (E1-E4) with clear position labels
Every email body is under 100 words (excluding greeting, sign-off, and signature token)
Email Bison spintax format used: {option1|option2|option3} NOT {{RANDOM | ...}}
Personalization token is {FIRST_NAME} (Email Bison format), not {{firstName}}
No em dashes or en dashes anywhere in any email
No signal references in copy (no mentions of fundraising, growth, hiring, job postings)
First name is the ONLY personalization variable — no company name, industry, or city merge fields in body
{SENDER_EMAIL_SIGNATURE} token present after sign-off in every email
Subject line variants provided for E1 and E3 (at least 2 per position)
E2 is threaded (reply to E1) — either empty subject or Re: prefix noted
E4 is threaded (reply to E3) — either empty subject or Re: prefix noted
CTA progression visible: E1 permission-based, E2 soft bump, E3 lower friction, E4 redirect/breakup
E3 pivots to a DIFFERENT value lever than E1 (not same angle reworded)
Case study data (15 hours/week, 8 client accounts) appears in at least one email
No spam trigger words (free, guarantee, act now, limited time, click here, buy now, discount, urgent, 100%, risk-free)
Spintax applied to greetings, transitions, CTAs, and sign-offs (minimum 3 options per block)
Day offsets specified for each email position

evals.name: local-service-instantly-with-strategy
evals.prompt:
Write a cold email sequence for BrightSmile Dental Marketing, a dental marketing agency in Austin, TX that builds patient acquisition systems for dental practices.

Strategy context:
- ICP: Dental practice owners, 3-20 employees, Texas and surrounding states
- Pain points: Relying on insurance referrals that pay less every year, spending $3K+/mo on Google Ads with no tracking, losing new patients to the practice down the street with better online reviews
- Offer: Done-for-you patient acquisition system ($2,500/mo)
- Value lever: Make money (more new patients)
- Case study: Helped Austin Family Dental go from 12 to 47 new patients/month in 90 days
- Messaging angles: Pain Dagger (insurance dependency), Proof Machine (12 to 47 patients), Value Gift (free Google presence audit)
- Tone: 3/5 formality, friendly, knowledgeable, not salesy
- CTA strategy: Lead magnet (free audit)
- Front-end offer: Complimentary Google presence audit
- Sequencer: Instantly
- Sender: Marcus Rivera, Founder
- Campaign name: brightsmile-dental
evals.expected_output: 4-email sequence with Instantly spintax format, proper threading, platform-specific tokens
expectations: Exactly 4 emails produced (E1-E4)
Every email body under 100 words
Instantly spintax format used: {{RANDOM | option1 | option2 | option3}} NOT {option1|option2|option3}
Personalization token is {{firstName}} (Instantly format), not {FIRST_NAME}
No em dashes or en dashes anywhere
No signal references in copy
First name is the ONLY personalization variable
Subject line variants provided for E1 and E3
E1 CTA is lead-magnet oriented (offers audit or resource, NOT asking for a call)
Case study data (12 to 47 new patients, 90 days) appears in at least one email
No spam trigger words
Spintax has 3+ options per block
E3 pivots value lever (if E1 = make money, E3 = save money or save time)
No links in Email 1
Threading info specified (E2 replies to E1, E4 replies to E3)

evals.name: standalone-no-strategy-file
evals.prompt:
Write a cold email sequence for my company. Here's what you need to know:
- We're a fractional CFO firm called Meridian Partners targeting Series A SaaS startups
- We helped a fintech extend their runway by 9 months and then led their $18M Series B
- Retainer is $8K-15K/mo
- Target: SaaS founders and CEOs, 20-100 employees
- Tone: professional but not stiff, 3-4 formality
- CTA: book a strategy call
- Sequencer: Email Bison
- Sender: David Park, Managing Partner
evals.expected_output: 4-email sequence written from standalone context (no strategy workspace file), using the provided details correctly
expectations: Exactly 4 emails produced without asking for a strategy file
Every email body under 100 words
Email Bison spintax format used: {option1|option2|option3}
{FIRST_NAME} token used (Email Bison)
{SENDER_EMAIL_SIGNATURE} present after sign-off
No em dashes or en dashes
Tone matches executive audience — no overly casual language, no slang
The $18M Series B case study is featured in social proof
High-ticket price ($8K-15K/mo) is NOT mentioned in the email copy (price belongs on call, not in cold email)
E1 CTA is permission-based (not asking for calendar commitment)
No spam trigger words
Subject lines sound internal/colleague-like, not promotional
First name is the ONLY personalization variable

evals.name: edge-case-bare-minimum-prompt
evals.prompt: Write cold emails for my business.
evals.expected_output: The skill should NOT write a full sequence with made-up details. It should ask for the minimum context it needs.
expectations: Does NOT generate a full email sequence with fabricated details
Asks about the target audience (title, industry, company size)
Asks about the offer (what the business does)
Asks about tone preference
Asks about CTA strategy (book a call vs lead magnet)
Asks which sequencer (Email Bison or Instantly)
Mentions that having a strategy file from the strategy skill would be helpful
Does not overwhelm with too many questions at once

evals.name: rlm-cta-with-strategy-context
evals.prompt:
Write a cold email sequence for Consulti AI, a cold email automation platform.

Strategy context:
- ICP: Agency founders, staffing firm owners, MSP owners, 5-50 employees, US
- Pain points: Feast-or-famine client cycle, spending hours writing outreach emails by hand, inconsistent pipeline
- Offer: AI-powered cold email platform, $97/mo starter, AI writes sequences from website URL, 15M+ verified leads delivered daily
- Value lever: Save time + Make money
- Case study: 2,500+ agencies on platform, top users seeing 5.4% reply rates (3x industry average)
- Messaging angles: Pain Dagger (feast/famine), RLM Sequences (ran their website through AI), RLM Leads (pulled verified leads)
- Tone: 2/5 formality, confident, direct, helpful
- CTA strategy: Lead magnet (reverse lead magnet — offer AI-generated sequences or verified leads)
- Front-end offer: AI-generated email sequences specific to their business
- Sequencer: Email Bison
- Sender: Jay Feldman, Founder
- Campaign name: consulti-agencies
evals.expected_output: 4-email sequence with RLM-style CTAs that offer to send something rather than book a call
expectations: Exactly 4 emails produced
Every email body under 100 words
Email Bison spintax format: {option1|option2|option3}
{FIRST_NAME} and {SENDER_EMAIL_SIGNATURE} tokens present
No em dashes or en dashes
E1 CTA is a reverse lead magnet offer (offers to send sequences/leads, NOT asks for a call)
RLM angle present: references running their website through AI or pulling leads for them
No links in Email 1
Pricing ($97/mo) is NOT in E1 body (save for later emails or call)
Social proof (2,500+ agencies, 5.4% reply rate) woven into at least one email
E3 pivots to a genuinely different angle than E1
No spam trigger words
First name is the ONLY personalization variable
Day offsets specified
